courseTitle: Floor Plan Drawing — Municipal Building Standards
courseDescription: Complete guide to creating professional floor plans for municipal submission. Covers walls, doors, windows, rooms, dimensions, annotations, building code compliance, and practical field measurements for residential and commercial building permits.

practicalExamples.title: Residential Apartment Floor Plan
steps: Start with field measurements using laser distance meter
Verify room dimensions at multiple points for accuracy
Design layout per NBC (National Building Code) requirements
Include minimum room sizes: Living (12 sq m), Bedroom (10 sq m), Kitchen (6 sq m)
Ensure proper ventilation and natural light requirements

buildingCodeRequirements.minimumWindowArea: 10% of floor area for natural light

practicalExamples.title: Commercial Office Floor Plan
practicalExamples.description: Open office layout with meeting rooms and facilities
steps: Measure existing space with total station or laser scanner
Include fire separation walls as per fire code

practicalExamples.title: Educational Institution Floor Plan
practicalExamples.description: School classroom layout with corridors and facilities
steps: Measure building footprint with GPS-enabled devices
Design classroom sizes per student capacity
Include proper egress corridors and stairs

courseObjectives: Create accurate floor plans showing room layouts and circulation
Include all building elements: walls, doors, windows, stairs, and fixtures
Add proper dimensions, room names, and building code annotations
Produce municipal-compliant floor plans ready for building permit submission

phases.phaseTitle: Setup — Floor Plan Standards and Layers
phases.phaseDescription: Prepare drawing environment with proper units, layers, and architectural standards.

steps.title: Configure Drawing Units and Scale
steps.subtitle: Set up for architectural floor plan requirements
steps.why: Floor plans typically use 1:50 or 1:100 scale and require millimeter precision for accurate construction.
instructions: Type UNITS and set Type to Decimal, Insertion Scale to Millimeters
Set Precision to 0 for whole millimeters (no decimal places needed)
Set LIMITS to accommodate building footprint (e.g., 0,0 to 20000,15000 for 20m x 15m building)
Set annotation scale to 1:50 for detailed plans or 1:100 for overall plans
Save as Floor_Plan_Template.dwt for future projects

steps.title: Create Floor Plan Layer System
steps.subtitle: Organize all architectural elements
steps.why: Proper layer organization is essential for controlling visibility, printing different plan types, and municipal review.
instructions: Create structural layers: WALL (Red, 0.40mm), COLUMN (Cyan, 0.50mm), BEAM (Yellow, 0.35mm)
Create opening layers: DOOR (Magenta, 0.25mm), WINDOW (Blue, 0.25mm), STAIR (Green, 0.30mm)
Create fixture layers: FIXTURES (White, 0.20mm), PLUMBING (Brown, 0.25mm), ELECTRICAL (Red, 0.18mm)
Create annotation layers: TEXT (White, 0.18mm), DIMENSION (Yellow, 0.18mm), HATCH (Dark Gray, 0.13mm)
Create reference layers: GRID (Gray, 0.09mm), CENTERLINE (Green, 0.13mm), FURNITURE (Light Gray, 0.15mm)
Create special layers: DEMOLITION (Red, 0.25mm, Dashed), NEW_CONSTRUCTION (Green, 0.40mm)

phases.phaseDescription: Establish the building grid and place structural elements.

steps.title: Create Column Grid Layout
steps.subtitle: Establish structural grid system
steps.why: Column grid provides the structural framework and reference for all building elements.
instructions: Set current layer to GRID
Draw grid lines at regular intervals (typically 3m, 4m, or 5m spacing)
Use ARRAY to create multiple grid lines efficiently
Add grid labels (A, B, C along one axis, 1, 2, 3 along perpendicular axis)
Ensure grid extends beyond building footprint for clarity
Use different linetype for main grid vs. secondary grid if needed

steps.title: Place Structural Columns
steps.subtitle: Position columns at grid intersections
steps.why: Columns support the structure and must be precisely positioned according to structural design.
instructions: Set current layer to COLUMN
Draw column cross-section (typically square 300x300mm to 600x600mm)
Place columns at grid intersections as per structural design
Use ARRAY for regular column patterns
Add column identification marks if required
Show column sizes and reinforcement details in separate schedule if needed

steps.title: Add Beams and Structural Elements
steps.subtitle: Complete structural framework
steps.why: Beams and other structural elements complete the building's structural system.
instructions: Set current layer to BEAM
Draw beam lines connecting columns
Use OFFSET to show beam width (typically 200-300mm wide)
Show beam drops or variations in height if required
Add structural notes and specifications
Ensure structural elements don't conflict with architectural requirements

phases.phaseDescription: Draw walls and define room spaces.

steps.title: Draw Exterior Walls with Field Verification
steps.subtitle: Create building perimeter using measured dimensions
steps.why: Exterior walls define the building envelope and must match field measurements with building code compliance.
instructions: Set current layer to WALL
Field Method: Measure wall dimensions on-site using laser distance meter
Draw exterior wall centerlines using measured dimensions
Use OFFSET to create wall thickness (200-300mm for exterior walls)
Verify diagonal measurements for squareness (use MEASUREGEOM)
Use FILLET to create clean wall corners with proper radii
Add wall construction annotations (e.g., '250mm RCC wall')
Include fire rating if required by building code

practicalTip.title: Field Measurement Accuracy
practicalTip.text: Always measure each wall at least twice and at different heights. Record the average dimension. For irregular walls, take measurements at 0.5m intervals.

steps.title: Draw Interior Walls and Partitions
steps.subtitle: Define interior spaces
steps.why: Interior walls create room divisions and must follow functional and code requirements.
instructions: Continue with WALL layer for interior walls
Draw interior partition walls (typically 100mm to 150mm thick)
Use TRIM to clean up wall intersections
Ensure wall placement allows for proper room dimensions
Consider acoustic and fire rating requirements for partition types

steps.title: Add Wall Openings and Penetrations
steps.subtitle: Show openings for doors, windows, and services
steps.why: Wall openings must be properly shown and sized for doors, windows, and service penetrations.
instructions: Create openings in walls for doors and windows
Use RECTANG to define opening sizes, then TRIM wall lines
Add service penetrations for plumbing, electrical, and HVAC
Show lintel details above openings if required
Add opening size annotations and specifications

phases.phaseDescription: Add doors and windows with proper symbols and dimensions.

steps.title: Draw Doors and Door Swings
steps.subtitle: Show door types and opening directions
steps.why: Door symbols must show clear opening direction and type for circulation and code compliance.
instructions: Set current layer to DOOR
Draw door leaf (typically 35mm thick shown as rectangle)
Add door swing arc showing opening direction
Show door frame and hardware if required
Use different symbols for different door types (single, double, sliding)
Add door size annotations (e.g., '900x2100' for 900mm wide x 2100mm high)

steps.title: Draw Windows and Openings
steps.subtitle: Show window types and locations
steps.why: Windows must be properly shown with type, size, and opening method for natural light and ventilation.
instructions: Set current layer to WINDOW
Draw window openings in walls (typically 100-200mm deep shown)
Show window frame and glazing lines
Indicate opening method (sliding, casement, fixed) with appropriate symbols
Add window size annotations and type specifications
Ensure windows meet building code requirements for light and ventilation

phases.phaseDescription: Add stairs, elevators, and other vertical circulation elements.

steps.title: Draw Stairs and Landings
steps.subtitle: Show vertical circulation
steps.why: Stairs must be properly dimensioned and shown with direction indicators for safety and code compliance.
instructions: Set current layer to STAIR
Draw stair outline and landing areas
Use ARRAY to create individual treads (typically 250-300mm deep)
Add direction arrow showing 'UP' or 'DOWN' direction
Show handrail and guardrail locations if required
Add stair dimensions: width, riser height, tread depth
Include stair identification and fire rating if required

steps.title: Add Elevators and Lifts
steps.subtitle: Show vertical transport systems
steps.why: Elevators must be clearly shown with dimensions and machine room locations.
instructions: Draw elevator car dimensions (typically 1500x2100mm for passenger elevator)
Show elevator doors and opening direction
Add machine room location if applicable
Include elevator capacity and type annotations
Show pit and overhead clearance requirements if needed

phases.phaseDescription: Add plumbing fixtures, kitchen equipment, and built-in fixtures.

steps.title: Add Plumbing Fixtures
steps.subtitle: Show toilets, sinks, and drainage
steps.why: Plumbing fixtures must be properly located and sized for building code compliance.
instructions: Set current layer to PLUMBING
Draw toilet fixtures (typically 600x1000mm for standard toilet)
Add sinks, urinals, and other fixtures as required
Show drainage connections and vent pipes if required
Add fixture type and size annotations
Ensure fixtures meet accessibility requirements (ADA/local codes)

steps.title: Add Kitchen and Built-in Fixtures
steps.subtitle: Show built-in equipment and fixtures
steps.why: Built-in fixtures and equipment must be properly shown for space planning and utilities.
instructions: Set current layer to FIXTURES
Draw kitchen counters, cabinets, and built-in appliances
Show shelving, storage, and other built-in elements
Add laboratory or specialized fixtures if applicable
Use HATCH to differentiate fixture types
Add fixture specifications and clearance requirements

phases.phaseDescription: Add dimensions, room names, and technical annotations.

steps.title: Add Dimension Strings with Verification
steps.subtitle: Complete technical dimensioning with field verification notes
steps.why: Accurate dimensions with verification notes are essential for construction quality and municipal approval.
instructions: Set current layer to DIMENSION
Add overall building dimensions with tolerance notes (±10mm)
Dimension individual room sizes with clear annotations
Add field verification notes: 'Measured on-site 2025-01-15'
Use DIMCONTINUE for efficient chained dimensions
Add structural grid dimensions and column locations
Include construction tolerances where critical
Verify dimensions don't overlap and are clearly readable

steps.title: Add Room Names and Areas
steps.subtitle: Identify all spaces
steps.why: Room names and areas are required for building permits and space planning.
instructions: Set current layer to TEXT
Add room names (e.g., 'LIVING ROOM', 'BEDROOM 1', 'KITCHEN')
Calculate and annotate room areas (e.g., '15.5 m²')
Use consistent text height (typically 3mm at print scale)
Position text to avoid overlapping with dimensions or fixtures
Add floor level identification if multiple floors

steps.title: Add Building Code Annotations
steps.subtitle: Include required code information
steps.why: Building code annotations are mandatory for municipal permit approval.
instructions: Add occupancy load calculations for each room
Show required means of egress and travel distances
Add fire rating requirements for walls and doors
Include accessibility compliance notes
Add construction material specifications if required
Show required clearances and minimum dimensions

phases.phaseDescription: Complete the floor plan with final details and required elements.

steps.title: Add Floor Finishes and Materials
steps.subtitle: Show flooring types and patterns
steps.why: Floor finishes may be required for specific areas and affect construction details.
instructions: Set current layer to HATCH
Use different hatch patterns for different floor materials
Add material legends and specifications
Show special flooring requirements (wet areas, clean rooms)
Include flooring transitions and thresholds
Add finish schedule if multiple types used

steps.title: Add North Arrow and Scale
steps.subtitle: Essential plan elements
steps.why: North arrow and scale are mandatory for all floor plan submissions.
instructions: Insert professional north arrow pointing to true north
Add scale bar appropriate to drawing scale (1:50 or 1:100)
Include written scale notation
Place elements in non-obtrusive but visible locations

steps.title: Create Title Block and Drawing Information
steps.subtitle: Complete submission documentation
steps.why: Title block provides essential project identification and drawing information.
instructions: Create title block with project name, floor plan title, scale, date
Include drawing number, floor level, and revision information
Add designer, checker, and approval signatures
Include municipal reference numbers and permit details
Add drawing index and related sheet references

steps.title: Final Municipal Compliance Check
steps.subtitle: Verify all requirements with field verification
steps.why: Municipalities require field-verified dimensions and complete code compliance for floor plan approval.
instructions: Verify all dimensions match field measurements within tolerance
Check room names and areas with calculated verification
Ensure minimum room sizes per NBC requirements
Confirm accessibility requirements (ADA/local codes) are fully met
Verify egress routes and travel distances
Check natural light and ventilation compliance
Prepare field measurement verification sheet
Create PDF submission with construction-ready layers
complianceChecklist: Room areas meet minimum size requirements
Two means of egress from all spaces
